title: mesosphaerum subrotundum
intro: Mesosphaerum subrotundum, also known as Brazilian peppertree or 'candelilla', is a shrub-like plant native to South America, particularly in Brazil. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Mesosphaerum subrotundum include relief from fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Mesosphaerum subrotundum can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
